design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate through demon-infested levels, defeat enemies with collected weapons, discover hidden treasures via jumping and shooting, progress through sequential stages with increasing difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited weapon durability/ammo), permadeath risk, pattern memorization of enemy placements and level hazards, mandatory second playthrough to access final boss
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Heroic knight Sir Arthur rescuing his beloved Princess Prin-Prin by fighting through hell itself, collecting increasingly powerful weapons and magical artifacts
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Capcom's arcade design philosophy: brutal difficulty as core value proposition, punishment-based progression requiring mastery through repetition, treasure chest gambling mechanic that can transform player into duck or elderly man as humorous failure state, mandatory replay requirement (complete levels 1-5 twice) to access true ending—difficulty is not optional but integral to narrative structure

difficulty.lives_system: Standard arcade lives system with progressive continues; player must manage limited resources across multiple attempts
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade-style continue mechanism; failure forces restart or credit insertion
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; levels must be completed in single runs; progression reset on death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal agency; difficulty is fixed and non-negotiable. Player agency exists only in weapon selection from discovered pickups and treasure chest gambling. Gold armor allows weapon charging for risk/reward resource management, but overall difficulty curve is designed by developers with no adjustment options

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system (various melee and projectile weapons discoverable mid-level); gold armor enables charged magical attacks; Master System version added persistent armor upgrades (helmets for new weapons/spells, chest armor for damage resistance, boots for speed increase)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Special weapon appears only on second playthrough of levels 1-5; special weapon is mandatory equipment to access final boss Lucifer's chamber. Level progression locked behind boss defeats (must defeat Beelzebub before final boss door opens)
replayability_hooks: Mandatory second playthrough with new special weapon availability
Multiple weapon types with distinct mechanics encourage different playstyles
Treasure chest gambling creates variable runs (weapon discovery, gold armor, or transformation penalty)
High difficulty encourages skill mastery and optimization attempts

world_building_techniques: Opening plot exposition: Princess Prin-Prin's soul stolen; players must journey through hell to restore souls
Environmental storytelling: eerie levels populated with undead and demonic creatures establishing hellish atmosphere
Character transformation Easter eggs: treasure chest encounters can turn Arthur into elderly man or duck, adding dark humor to failure states
Boss encounters as narrative progression: defeat of demons and Beelzebub as steps toward confronting evil Lucifer
Level design implying a vertical journey from mortal realm through increasingly hellish domains

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based encounters requiring memorization of attack sequences and safe positioning windows; each boss tests mastery of movement and weapon timing accumulated through level practice
notable_bosses: Beelzebub: mandatory defeat required to progress to final boss chamber
Lucifer: final antagonist encountered only after completing levels 1-5 twice and obtaining special weapon; represents ultimate challenge and narrative climax

faction_indicators: Princess Prin-Prin (victim requiring rescue; symbolic of nobility/innocence)
Demons and undead minions (enemy faction serving Lucifer)
danger_signals: Transformation into duck or elderly man: humorous punishment for greed/failure, indicates vulnerability
Mandatory second playthrough: game design communicates 'true ending requires mastery'
Beelzebub boss gate: power-check preventing under-leveled progression
Special weapon requirement for final boss: design communicates specific path/preparation needed
environmental_storytelling: Eerie level aesthetics establishing hellish setting
Progression through demon-infested terrain suggests descent into underworld
Treasure chest mechanics embedded in levels suggest hidden value in exploration
Enemy placement and density increasing across stages implies escalating danger
